scenarios.title: Talk about a turning point
scenarios.subtitle: A short role-play to practise reflecting on life-changing moments.
scenarios.studentRole: You meet a mentor for coffee. Tell him about a turning point that changed your life.
scenarios.goal: Describe a turning point and reflect on it using emphatic structures.
scenarios.openingLine: It's good to see you. So, tell me — was there a moment that really changed your direction in life?
scenarios.targetLanguage: 'It was not until...', 'Had I known...', inversion (Little did I realise...)

scenarios.title: Negotiate a compromise
scenarios.subtitle: A short role-play to practise negotiation and conditionals.
scenarios.studentRole: You need to negotiate a project deadline and budget with your manager.
scenarios.goal: Negotiate a change at work using polite suggestions and conditions.
scenarios.openingLine: Thanks for coming in. I'd like to discuss the project schedule — I think we need some changes.

scenarios.title: Debate AI at work
scenarios.subtitle: A short role-play to practise giving opinions on technology.
scenarios.studentRole: A colleague has strong views on AI. Share your opinion on AI in the workplace.
scenarios.goal: Give and support an opinion using impersonal passive structures.
scenarios.openingLine: Honestly, I think AI is going to replace half our jobs in a decade. What's your take?
scenarios.targetLanguage: 'It is widely believed that...', 'It has been argued that...', passive voice

scenarios.title: Give a business update
scenarios.subtitle: A short role-play to practise reporting and the passive.
scenarios.studentRole: You report last quarter's results to a director in a meeting.
scenarios.goal: Report results and news using reporting passives.
scenarios.openingLine: Let's begin. Could you give me a brief update on last quarter's performance?
scenarios.targetLanguage: 'It was announced that...', 'It has been confirmed that...', reporting passives

scenarios.title: Discuss a global issue
scenarios.subtitle: A short role-play to practise discussing social problems.
scenarios.studentRole: A classmate wants to discuss inequality. Share your views and possible solutions.
scenarios.goal: Discuss a global issue and propose solutions persuasively.
scenarios.openingLine: I've been reading about global inequality and it really worries me. What do you think we should do?
scenarios.targetLanguage: 'It has been argued that...', 'Only by ... can we...', 'In order to...'

scenarios.title: Describe a work of art
scenarios.subtitle: A short role-play to practise describing art and emotions.
scenarios.studentRole: At a gallery, a fellow visitor asks about a painting you love. Describe it.
scenarios.goal: Describe a painting and the feelings it creates.
scenarios.openingLine: That painting really caught my eye. What do you make of it?
scenarios.targetLanguage: 'It depicts...', 'The use of ... creates...', narrative tenses for reactions

scenarios.title: Present your research
scenarios.subtitle: A short role-play to practise formal presentations.
scenarios.studentRole: You present findings to a colleague and answer her questions.
scenarios.goal: Present findings and answer questions using formal language.
scenarios.openingLine: Thanks for the presentation. So, what do your findings actually suggest?
scenarios.targetLanguage: 'The data suggests that...', 'It is worth noting that...', 'In conclusion...'

scenarios.title: Reflect on your B2 progress
scenarios.subtitle: A short role-play to review everything you've learned at B2.
scenarios.studentRole: You talk with a course advisor about how far you've come and your next goal.
scenarios.goal: Reflect on your progress and your next goal using emphatic structures.
scenarios.openingLine: Well done on completing B2! Looking back, how do you feel about your progress?
scenarios.targetLanguage: 'Not only have I...', 'It was not until...', 'My goal now is to...'
